planner.description: Produces per-ticket implementation plans working within the existing architecture. Read-only. Never makes architecture decisions.

architect.description: Designs overall solution structure, chooses technologies, defines high-level architecture, maintains architecture docs.

developer.description: Implements production code following AGENTS.md standards and the plan.

tester.description: Writes and maintains unit tests using the testing skill.

reviewer.description: Read-only code review: checks standards, patterns, tests, and architecture compliance.

cleanup.description: Final cleanup pass: add minimal instructive comments, remove AI conversational artifacts, organize imports, fix whitespace.

self-improver.description: Read-only introspection of the setup skill. Audits templates, detects inconsistencies, generates improvement tickets. /self-improve to invoke.

ticket.description: Start work on a ticket, e.g. /ticket TICKET1
ticket.template: Read docs/Tasks/{arg}-*/requirement.md and docs/Tasks/{arg}-*/status.md (if exists). Follow the workflow in AGENTS.md. Use sub-agents: @planner -> @architect -> @developer -> @reviewer -> @developer (if issues found) -> @tester -> @developer (if tests fail) -> @cleanup. Present the plan to the user after @planner, show architecture after @architect, show changes after @developer, show review results after @reviewer, show test results after @tester, and ask about commit at the end. After completing, update status.md with progress.

self-improve.description: Run full introspection on the setup skill. Audits templates, placeholders, paths, and structure. Auto-fixes mechanical issues, creates improvement tickets for structural ones.
self-improve.template: Run a full introspection pass on the setup skill in .opencode/skills. Audit templates, placeholders, paths, and directory structure for inconsistencies. Auto-fix any purely mechanical issues (typos, broken paths, stale references). For anything structural or requiring a design decision, create an improvement ticket under docs/Tasks/ instead of editing directly. Report a summary of fixes applied and tickets created.
